items.title: The local, no-account RustyRed node
items.acceptance: Starting the local node and running the CLI in a repo opens or creates a project-scoped store on disk, verified by a write and a read-back over the local MCP endpoint with no tenant and no token; a second repo opens an isolated store; the node is reachable only on loopback.
verification_evidence: Validated carry_data_dir derives distinct project-scoped stores for two source roots.
Validated live loopback MCP write/read on 127.0.0.1:18380 using tenantless upsert_note and observe calls.
Validated scripts/theorem carry data-dir and mcp-url wrappers.
items.deferral_reason: Not yet run as a full theorem carry up lifecycle from a clean repo; local MCP and data-dir contracts were proven directly.
validation: Start with THEOREM_CARRY_HOME on a temp dir and THEOREM_MCP_URL on loopback.
Call tools/call upsert_note with no tenant or token, then observe or recall it back.
Repeat from a second temp repo and prove the derived data_dir differs.
items.risk: A single global node can accidentally mix project memory unless repo identity is part of the data-dir or project slug.

items.title: Session-activity capture
items.acceptance: A Claude Code session in a repo produces graph nodes for the tools used and files edited, each with a timestamp and session provenance and linked to the project; a Codex session in the same repo produces the same; a forced failure with the node down leaves the session uninterrupted.
verification_evidence: Stub MCP captured a PostToolUse payload through carry-capture.sh as upsert_note with session, tool, file, repo, and metadata.
Installer fixtures registered Claude and Codex SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, and Stop managed hooks.
items.deferral_reason: Not yet validated by running real Claude Code and Codex sessions end-to-end.
validation: Run hook JSON fixtures for PostToolUse and UserPromptSubmit through carry-capture.sh with a stub MCP endpoint.
Run the same script with an unreachable endpoint and assert exit 0.
Inspect written upsert_note metadata for event, session, repo, file, tool, and timestamp fields.
items.risk: Codex and Claude hook payloads differ; capture must be liberal and fail-open.

items.title: Session-memory serve and compaction survival
items.acceptance: Starting a new session in a repo with prior history injects a bounded capsule containing prior decisions and touched-file context in addition to the code map; calling the retrieval tool returns relevant session memory; after a forced compaction, re-pulling via the tool returns the pre-compaction state and the session continues oriented.
verification_evidence: carry-serve.sh stub returned a bounded Session memory capsule from observe recall_results.
session_start.sh local mode injected carry memory and the existing compute_code code neighborhood without THEOREM_TENANT_ID.
Tenantless live local MCP write/read validated the seed and retrieve path.
items.deferral_reason: Forced compaction survival was not exercised in a live agent session.
validation: Seed a local note with upsert_note.
Run session_start.sh with no THEOREM_TENANT_ID and local MCP env.
Assert stdout includes a bounded session-memory capsule.
Call observe with query and assert the seeded memory returns.
items.risk: The existing session_start.sh exits early without THEOREM_TENANT_ID; local mode must bypass that hosted-only gate without breaking hosted behavior.

items.title: Consolidation on session end
items.acceptance: After a session ends, its raw observations are consolidated into a durable summary node linked to the touched files and the decisions, and the next session's injected capsule reflects the consolidation rather than the raw observation stream.
verification_evidence: Stop, SessionEnd, and PreCompact paths in carry-capture.sh write a session_summary marker through upsert_note.
Source review found rustyred-thg-memory consolidation APIs but no dedicated public carry MCP consolidate tool to call directly.
items.deferral_reason: Raw-observation consolidation into a lower-priority durable MemorySummary remains partial until the actual public MCP consolidation operation is exposed or confirmed.
validation: Run Stop hook fixture after multiple captured observations.
Call memory.consolidate or the MCP memory consolidate surface against local node.
Read back a MemorySummary node or observe capsule that includes the summary.
Assert raw observations are archived or lower priority.
items.risk: The public MCP tool list exposes memory lifecycle through plugin operations, not a dedicated carry endpoint; script must call the actual advertised operation.

items.title: One-command install
items.acceptance: On a clean machine, one command installs the product, and with no further configuration a subsequent Claude Code or Codex session in a repo is captured and a later session in that repo is served from the local node; uninstall removes the registered hooks and leaves the agents working.
verification_evidence: packaging/carry/install.sh install, status, and uninstall passed against temp Claude and Codex config files.
Managed hook entries cover SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, and Stop for both agents, and uninstall removes only managed entries.
packaging/carry/README.md documents install, uninstall, status, and the no-tenant/no-token requirement.
items.deferral_reason: Clean-machine install and real follow-up agent session were not run.
validation: Assert Claude and Codex hook manifests include session start, prompt, post-tool, and stop capture commands.
Run uninstall and assert the managed block is removed.
No tenant, token, or network env is required.
items.risk: Installer must not overwrite existing user hooks; it needs managed blocks or a reversible backup.
